content: I was photographing sunset shots on Sohna Road from 6:30 PM to 9:00 PM on March 17. I was capturing the light on the hills near the golf course. I have over 40 geotagged photos on my phone from that time. I returned home at 9:15 PM. I had no idea about the accident until the police came to my garage the next day. My wife’s car — the blue XUV700 — was parked in the garage. I didn’t drive it that day. I use my own black Honda City. The headlight was broken, but I thought it had been damaged two weeks ago during the monsoon pothole incident. I didn’t repair it because I was busy with assignments. I swear I didn’t go near Sector 45 that evening.
